Question: What about those who take money to read holy books?
Answer: Look, money and women are the two ways they can be caught. I request the government to investigate these people, perhaps by using women as agents. Then it will become clear what they do and don't do. It is necessary to investigate them, and then they will be caught.

Video Title: L32 सत्य में रहने वाले भक्तो को निंदा-अपमान मिलेगा-- विष्णु भगवान संहार के लिए कल्कि रूप धारण करेंगे
Question: I don't have a question, I just want to say that you are helping us focus our attention on the ultimate truth.
Answer: Look, mother, I am an ordinary human like you. I pray to God that pure souls like you who want to walk on the path of Dharma receive God's grace. May the Lord show you the right path and may you progress on the path of Dharma. That is all.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: When the nuclear war happens and atomic bombs are dropped on India's enemy countries, how will the devotees living there be saved? There will be poisonous gases and destruction.
Answer: According to the Malika, devotees who stay on the righteous path, take refuge in the Lord, and follow the Malika will be saved. Through the Sudharma Sangh, 16 'mandals' (zones) will be formed, and people from outside India can also join. These devotees will be purified by following practices like reading the Srimad Bhagavatam, performing Trisandhya, chanting the 16 names, and the Dashavatara. Before the nuclear war or the catastrophe of the five elements, God will save them through his divine power. The complete responsibility for their protection has been assigned to the Lord and the deities. So, there is no need to worry. The only necessity is to follow the path we have described. I don't know what will happen to those who cannot follow it, but those who do will be safe.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: I have two questions from a devotee. First, are the Vishwa Sanatan Dharma and the Sudharma Mahasangh the same, or are they different? Second, in a previous session, you said something like, 'whether I am here tomorrow or not,' implying your death. Why do you say such things? It makes us sad.
Answer: Regarding your second question, human life has no guarantee or stability; it is a divine gift from God. We can continue to live in this world as long as our actions and purity are correct. If there is any mistake in my words or deeds, I will also be judged. More importantly, those who take refuge in God have no attachment to their life, as they are always in God's meditation. That's why such words sometimes come to mind. I don't want to have such emotional talks, but it happens on its own. If you all feel hurt by this, I humbly ask for your forgiveness.   Regarding your first question about Sudharma Mahasangh and Vishwa Sanatan Dharma: these two organizations are different stages of the same path. It's like a good student who goes from school to college, then to a national university, and then to an international university. The goal is the same. In Sanatan Dharma, devotees first gain knowledge about the Malika, Sudharma Sangh, Bhavishya Purana, the establishment of Dharma, and God. After a year or two, when their minds become completely pure and free of conflict, they will work through the Sudharma Mahasangh. They will become 'Sudharma,' which means they will have the behavior and actions of a deity. When the Lord arrives, He himself will establish the Sudharma Mahasangh. The most devoted people from the Sanatan Dharma, who have surrendered to the Lord and the Malika in mind, love, and devotion, will be close to the Lord and become members of the Sudharma Mahasangh.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: In the Dvapara Yuga, Kansa made many attempts to harm Lord Krishna. Similarly, in this era, will the Kalki avatar also have to face such troubles?
Answer: This is a very good question. The Malika states that Lord Kalki will be born as a human child but will possess immense power. At will, he can create or destroy the seven great continents (Sapta Dvipa) in a moment. A mere glance from his eyes will radiate the light of a crore suns. The entire universe will tremble from the force of his breath. Every pore of Lord Kalki's body will contain crores of universes. Though he will look like a child, his actions will be that of the Supreme Being (Brahman). He will be born with 64 'kalas' (divine arts) to establish Dharma. Therefore, it is impossible for anyone to harm him. In previous eras, the demons had divine powers and weapons. In this age, the demons are in human form, and Lord Kalki will eliminate them through subtle means. No one will be able to cause him any harm. If anyone even tries, the Lord will finish them without anyone ever knowing. He has all the power and needs no help.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: During World War 3, will there be studies or not? Will online classes and such things happen, or will they all stop?
Answer: You should focus on your studies now. Discipline yourself in your studies and also in your devotion. The war will happen when it is destined to happen. At that time, everything from schools to computers will be destroyed. There will be no government or administration. In that era, people will follow the Dharma Shastras (scriptures of righteousness). So, for the next four to five years, study well, get good marks, make your parents happy, and also read the scriptures of Dharma. We will think about that time when it comes. To think about it now is not good for your mind. Leave those thoughts, focus on your studies, and chant the Lord's name.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: Lord Vishnu Narayan is in his cosmic form (Virat Roop). Does he possess only a few 'kalas' (divine arts), or does he have all 64 'kalas'?
Answer: When Lord Vishnu incarnates, he comes to give happiness to the devotees of all four yugas, to fulfill their rights, and to grant them the fruits of their penance. In this age, Lord Vishnu himself will leave his abode for some time and incarnate on Earth as Lord Kalki. The Malika says that Mahavishnu, the resident of the ocean of milk, incarnates as Kalki. Lord Kalki's body will contain the power of Lord Rama on the right side and Lord Krishna on the left. Balarama (as Ananta) and Goddess Mahalakshmi will also be with him to fulfill the devotees' desires for peace and happiness. Lord Kalki will be the confluence of all 64 'kalas'. He will possess immense power and establish Dharma. He is a complete avatar, just as Lord Krishna was the greatest avatar on Earth. Lord Kalki is the avatar of Mahavishnu himself. His four brothers will always be with him, and they will carry out his will. He is a complete incarnation.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: In Satya Yuga, when dharma was most prevalent, the Lord took more avatars compared to Kali Yuga. But in Kali Yuga, sin is so widespread. Shouldn't the Lord take more avatars here to maintain the purity of dharma among humans? Why did he take fewer avatars?
Answer: The premise is incorrect. In Satya Yuga, the Lord had five avatars: Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, and Vamana. In Treta Yuga, there were two: Parashurama and Rama. In Dvapara Yuga, there were two, considering Balarama as an avatar alongside Lord Krishna. However, in Kali Yuga, the Lord has taken three avatars: Lord Buddha, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and Lord Kalki. The total duration of Kali Yuga is 432,000 years, but of this, only about 5,000 years will pass in this cycle. So, three avatars is not a small number. The most important point is that in Kali Yuga, the Lord comes to uplift the devotees. In other yugas, the avatars primarily engaged in destruction of evil. In this age, Lord Buddha preached dharma, non-violence, and love for all beings. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu spread the dharma of love and the chanting of the holy name. Lord Kalki will not preach, explain, or make disciples. He will not hold public gatherings to announce his arrival. He comes solely to uplift the devotees. The Malika was written for the salvation of devotees because in the modern age, people would lack scriptural knowledge and would be saved by listening to the Malika. Therefore, the three avatars in Kali Yuga are very significant.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: I have a question. This technology that is at such a high level these days, will this all be destroyed too?
Answer: Look, even during the Mahabharata war, metals like brass, iron, and gold were available and used. That was their technology. In this age, the most advanced technologies are supercomputers, computers, and radar systems. In the coming Satya Yuga, metals will still exist. However, the current technology is controlled by machines, and machines can fail. We see space missions, missiles, and even supercomputers failing. But in the age to come, the power of mantras will be supreme. The power of mantras will be thousands and millions of times greater than the power of any machine.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: The Satya Yuga that is coming, will it be like the Satya Yuga of King Harishchandra's time?
Answer: No, it will be even better. In the time of Harishchandra and Vishwamitra, there were kings who ruled. But in the coming Satya Yuga, the mortal realm will become like heaven (Swarg-lok). Lord Vishnu himself will rule, not any king. The development, peace, and happiness will be heavenly. All the deities and the five elements will be engaged in the service of the devotees. Devotees will experience infinite bliss and will be granted 'ichha mrityu' (the ability to choose one's time of death). There will be no sorrow because the Lord and Mahalakshmi themselves will provide for everyone. There will be no scarcity of anything. The entire earth will be transformed into heaven. The divine architect Vishwakarma will construct divine dwellings for devotees, and there will be divine food and divine bodies for humans. Trees will bear fruit year-round. There will only be happiness, peace, and a blissful atmosphere.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: Will there be a final king, and will people have the power of 'ichha mrityu' (choosing their time of death)?
Answer: There is no proof of a final king. Yes, people will have 'ichha mrityu'. Lord Vishnu is coming, and devotees will be able to leave their bodies at will. Even deities in heaven sometimes commit wrongdoings and are reborn on Earth due to a curse. However, the people who will exist in the new age will all be deities, sages, and saints. They will only perform good deeds. That is why we emphasize reading the Srimad Bhagavatam. A person who reads the Bhagavatam will hesitate a hundred times before telling a lie. They will not walk on the wrong path. They will have control over their anger, ego, and five senses. This is why the Vishwa Sanatan Dharma is promoting this path—to purify people and prepare them for that new age of peace and bliss.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: There are so many fake gurus, like those who claim to know the father of Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh, or those who claim they can make you meet Lord Kalki. How do people get trapped by such charlatans?
Answer: Those who truly seek God will find Him, and those who don't, won't. Those who spend their time with frauds will attain hell. The biggest problem in the world today is the lack of compassion in people's hearts. If they had compassion, they would help the poor, the miserable, and the needy instead of giving their money to these charlatans. There are so many poor people in India, yet people run after fake saints. But there are also true saints. I met a saint from Ayodhya who has nothing—no place to live, no ashram. He eats only if someone gives him food and doesn't ask anyone for anything. I offer millions of salutations to such saints. The world continues to exist because of them. A good and pure person will find a true saint. Conversely, a person who is himself fallen, sinful, unrighteous, and characterless will be drawn to a fake guru of a similar nature. It is a matter of like attracting like.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: There is so much corruption even in 'Gaushalas' (cow shelters). People who open them misuse the donation money, and the cows are left to starve and become emaciated. Why is this happening?
Answer: The cow is our mother; she is the abode of 33 crore deities. When a human grows up and can no longer drink his own mother's milk, it is the cow mother who nourishes us. In this Kali Yuga, people disrespect and even kill their own mothers, so it's not surprising they act against the cow mother as well. For such people, there is no destination other than destruction. Their time will come, and all of them will be destroyed. Time is slowly approaching.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: In the Malika written by Shri Achyutananda Ji Maharaj, is there any mention of Rishi Dayanand Saraswati, the founder of Arya Samaj?
Answer: Yes. Swami Dayanand Saraswati did great work for the protection of cows and for social awakening in India. His work is also a part of establishing Dharma. The Malika mentions many saints and great souls, including Anukulchandra, Dayanand Saraswati, Ramakrishna Paramahansa, Vivekananda, Sant Tukaram, and Tulsidas Maharaj. The Malika is vast, comprising 150,000 volumes, and not all are available. But it has written about the entire universe and these saints. It is also written that all these great souls who came to India and purified the land will be reborn. When Lord Kalki incarnates, they will take birth as ordinary humans. Later, when the Sudharma Sabha (Assembly of the Righteous) is established, the devotees will recognize who these people were in their past lives.

Video Title: L33 कलियुग के साधु - संत लोग कैसे चरित्र के होंगे?
Question: In Sanatan Vedic culture, Ashtanga Yoga is described as the path, but it's very difficult to follow in Kali Yuga. You say we can attain merit by listening to the Malika. However, many people are just stuck on questions about what will happen in the future, like in a world war, instead of focusing on their own and humanity's upliftment. Since 99% of people aren't progressing, what is the solution?
Answer: It is true that practicing the full Ashtanga Yoga is not possible in Kali Yuga, though the simplified yoga people practice today is a sign of the coming Satya Yuga. Regarding people's worries, there are two paths. The path of a person in Kali Yuga is to think about their own future, job, and happiness. The path of Dharma and the devotees of Satya Yuga is to think not about oneself, but about the welfare of the universe, the five elements, humanity, nature, and all living beings. Selfish people only think about their own future. Our goal is for society to change. A time will come when everyone in India, regardless of their current religion, will follow Sanatan Dharma and read the Srimad Bhagavatam. People may not accept it now, but a time will come when everyone living in India and the world will read it, perform Trisandhya, and sing bhajans. The solution is to spread the message of the Srimad Bhagavatam. The purification that comes from reading it is a hundred times greater than just listening to it. The vibrations from reading it will please the deities and the elements, bringing peace and happiness to the world. The Bhagavatam is always propagated before the Lord's arrival. This propagation is happening through you all. You should tell those you come in contact with. This is how the message will spread throughout the universe.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Pandit ji, how dangerous is this Omicron variant? And in 2022, will it cause devastation, or will there be riots again? What will cause destruction in 2022?
Answer: Look, whenever Dharma (righteousness) is to be established, God gives three indications, three warnings. He gives three warnings and three signs. This is the third variant. For this, I always say, follow the constitutional rules and the government's regulations. And the religious devotees, the pure people, should also follow our religious path. Those who are pure and wish to, should follow it. But we have written about the past. I don't want to comment much on this. I have said before that many pandemics will come by 2027. And in 2022, 2023, 24, 25, 26, up to 2027... it started in '19, so this stream will continue. How bad or good it will be, time will decide. But just like the five elements, this is also a stream in the establishment of Dharma. That's all. Those who remain in Dharma will face no problems. The problems are for those who live in sin.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: In his era, Lord Krishna killed many people with his Sudarshan Chakra, and they attained salvation (mukti). So now, when Lord Kalki kills people with the Sudarshan Chakra, will they also attain salvation?
Answer: Salvation and Vaikuntha (the celestial abode) are subject to the Lord's will. When the Lord annihilates people, it depends on Him. Because at the end of this yuga, the sins and virtues of all four yugas will be considered; the previous yuga was different. The Lord will destroy the sinners by taking on his annihilator form. Whether He will grant them salvation or not, I do not know, because this is not within my authority.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Pandit ji, please tell us a little about the name 'Madhav'. Where did this name come from? What is its significance? Please share what you know with us. As it is written in all the Puranas, Lord Kalki is coming. In the difficult times that are approaching, everyone will call upon Lord Kalki by his name, because everyone knows the tenth avatar will be the Kalki avatar. So, regarding the name 'Madhav', please guide us a little.
Answer: Look, it is written in the Malika: "Madhav, Madhav will be chanted everywhere." The entire universe will chant the name Madhav, Madhav. But not everyone will be able to recognize that Madhav, that is, the Lord. And in another place, Sachidananda has written, "The one who is Madhav now will be Kalki." When he performs the act of destruction, his name will be Kalki, but for now, his name is Madhav. By chanting the name Madhav, devotees will be saved. Along with that, chanting the Dashavatar Stotram and Durga Madhav Stuti is enough to set everything right. You had mentioned once that Goddess Durga wanted to give this name in the Dvapara Yuga. Due to lack of time, I couldn't elaborate, but I'll briefly say it now. In the Dvapara Yuga, when the Lord was born, Mother Bhagavati, Jogmaya, had named the Lord 'Madhav'. I had discussed this in a satsang one day. But the family priest, Garg Muni, named him 'Sri Krishna' during the naming ceremony. So, Mother Bhagavati, Goddess Durga, asked the Lord, "My Lord, I had kept this name for you for the Gopis and Gopas. Why was this name changed?" The Lord replied, "Look, Mother, this name is the most important one. In the Kali Yuga, devotees will be saved by this name. Of all the avatars I have taken, this name is the most powerful and full of devotion. So, at the end of Kali Yuga, when I take the Kalki avatar, devotees will be redeemed by worshipping the name Madhav. That is why I have reserved this name for the Kali Yuga."

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: What is 'Maya' (illusion)? And in the life we lead, what is Maya and what is not? Please tell us a little about this. We are devoted to the Lord's feet and want to understand Maya. To live this life, what is Maya? And what is the role of Yogmaya (the divine illusionary power, the Goddess) in our lives?
Answer: Look, in this world, whatever provides a person with worldly pleasure is Maya. For example, seeing something beautiful gives peace to the eyes. Eating good food gives peace to the tongue. Having wealth and prosperity gives a person sleep, happiness, and peace. A good house, good things, good land—whatever is a means of pleasure in this world is all transient; it has no permanence. Whatever things are not permanent are powers given by Maya. Because Maya is in them, and those things are not permanent. What is permanent is a person's devotion, surrender, stability, and tolerance, meaning patience. The one who practices truth, peace, compassion, forgiveness, and friendship in their life will not have Maya with them. Because if someone speaks the truth, they cannot earn through falsehood. It might be a little difficult, but there is no sickness in it. Family entanglements... the sacred relationship between a father and son is not Maya. But attachment to a child, the world, and a wife has a bit of Maya in it. Because a person will do anything for their child. Even if the child makes a mistake, they accept it; this is also a sign of Maya. So, Maya always gives sorrow, and Maya does not stay with a person forever. It is momentary. In this transient world, whatever we see—rivers, oceans, great roads, mountains—all are creations of Maya. A day will come when they will all dissolve. Only one thing can cut through Maya, and that is Dharma (righteousness). How does Dharma come? By practicing truth, peace, compassion, and forgiveness, a person accumulates Dharma. In this world, even our body is a form of Maya. A time will come when we will have to leave this body. But the soul is indestructible; it is not subject to Maya. Dharma is also not subject to Maya. Both of them together will go with the soul and, after all considerations, will place it in a good state. So, Maya is the cause of sorrow. And Dharma is for the meaningful purpose of human life. A person sometimes grieves in Maya: someone died, their business suffered a loss, they didn't get good results in their education. This is all under Maya's influence, which is why a person grieves. It's necessary for a person to know that the things they are striving for are not permanent. So, to stay away from Maya means whatever happens in the world, don't worry about what will or will not happen for us. Remain constantly surrendered to God, chant His name, whether you get food or not, whether you have a job or not. God will surely feed, provide for, and protect His devotees. That is His responsibility, not ours. Because of Maya, we think we will do it ourselves, and when we can't, we feel sorrow. This is how the world works. But a truly knowledgeable person is not disturbed by this. They walk in the path given by God. It's not necessary to be happy when happiness comes, and it's not necessary to be sad when sorrow comes. This is the greatest way to cut through Maya. And regarding the power of Yogmaya in the establishment of Dharma: in every age, for the destruction of demons, Yogmaya comes. In this age, Mahavishnu and Yogmaya will work together to establish Dharma. Mahamaya will carry out the destruction, and the Lord will save the devotees. If needed, where Mahamaya's work is not enough, Mahavishnu will use His power to carry out the destruction—saving the devotees on one side and destroying the sinners on the other. So, Yogmaya (Durga) and Madhav will both carry out the work of destruction. This is written in the Malika.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Could you please explain the meaning of 'daya' (compassion), which arises from truth and peace?
Answer: Truth, peace, and mercy... The meaning of 'daya' (compassion) is this: Look, man is a creature upon whom 560 million living beings depend. Because man has hands, feet, and knowledge, he can produce his own food and also eat it. He also has the power to distribute it, whereas other beings do not have such means. So, for all living beings, for the world of humans and the world of animals, it is necessary for man to have compassion. This means not killing anyone, not taking a life, not harming anyone, not forcefully taking someone's property, money, or resources. One should not interfere with what belongs to others. A person who has compassion thinks, "Why should I harm them or do something bad to them?" This is a sign of a compassionate nature. And the greatest compassion is when you see poor, miserable, handicapped, or disabled people or hungry animals, to give them food. This comes under compassion. And sometimes it might happen that a son, daughter, wife, or family member makes a mistake, destroys some of our property, or loses some small item. Forgive them. Because what is destined to happen, happens, and what is not, does not. We just get troubled in between, being subject to Maya. So, having affection, care, and love for everyone is a sign of compassion.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: When we go to help a needy person, should we lovingly explain things to them and help them find employment, or should we help them with food? Which of these two is considered true compassion?
Answer: Look, help people as much as they need. It is not necessary to give more than what is needed. Always observe this: teach those who are capable of working how to do so. Help those who are unable to work. And for those who will never be able to work, it is good to help them regularly. But for that, you must have the necessary resources yourself. Okay?

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: I saw on YouTube that there might be a war with China or an alien attack in 2022. Is this true?
Answer: A very good question. Today I was seeing some people talking about 2022 and an attack on China. People have also posted some videos about this on YouTube. Look, nothing like that is going to happen in 2022. This alien attack is not going to happen. It will never happen. Human society will itself reap the fruits of its own actions in the coming time. No aliens are coming from any other planet. No such attack is going to happen. This is just to mislead people. There is no truth in it. It will never happen.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Father, I have a question in my mind. When the final time comes, how will Lord Kalki gather all the devotees?
Answer: All the devotees will be gathered through the Malika. The Malika will be propagated, and pure people like you who are following the Malika will spread its message to good people in your villages and cities. Those who were saints, sages, and ascetics in their previous lives will be drawn to the Malika. By following the Malika, just as you are in this satsang today, they too will join you. In this way, all the devotees from all over India and the universe will be gathered. On one hand, the work of destruction will proceed, and on the other, the gathering of devotees will continue. Both will happen simultaneously. The work of destruction will continue, but the devotees will be gathered. The devotees will remain in the stream of chanting the Lord's name and kirtan, and they will be saved. Nothing will happen to the devotees. As the sinners are destroyed, gradually, more than three-fourths of the people will be wiped out. Those who remain will be the devotees of the Malika. Such a time will come. This work is proceeding with the Lord's blessings, and you all are also following the Malika. Slowly, the remaining devotees will also join.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: In every age, there were many wicked people who greatly troubled the good people. In the coming Satya Yuga, will the Lord prevent such people from being born, or what will happen to them? Could you tell us something about that?
Answer: The work of Sanatana Dharma that has now begun... every person who reads the Srimad Bhagavatam daily, practices truth, peace, and compassion, performs Trisandhya (prayers at three junctures of the day), chants the 16 names, and always speaks the truth... such people will be created through the Malika, and they will be sent for the Satya Yuga. All these demons, the unrighteous, the evil-natured humans—not a single one of them will remain. There will be no unrighteous people. Not a single person who kills animals will remain on this earth, not one. Because it will be the Age of Truth. And the people who remain will all live with brotherhood, like one family, as per our Sanatana Dharma's principle of 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam'—the whole world is one family. Everyone will help everyone. There will be peace, purity, and cooperation among all people. Everyone will have the means to eat, drink, and live. Not a single sinner will remain. And at that time, if anyone commits a sin or tells a lie, they will die. They won't be able to survive. Unlike the current Kali Yuga, where people sin as much as they want and accumulate as much wealth as they want. One person doesn't have money for medicine, while others have crores deposited in the bank. They die, and no one knows where that money goes, but the poor remain poor, and the rich get richer. This is why the Lord has come to establish Dharma. When God comes, He makes everyone equal and sees them all in the stream of the Vedas. That is why we propagate the stream of the Vedas for you—the true stream of Sanatana Dharma that can save you is with you. Those who hold onto the Vedas and the Lord's name will be in the Satya Yuga, where there will be no sinners, only good people. If anyone commits a sin, they will be gone.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: We have heard from many saints who have told the future and spoken about the end of the Kali Yuga, but none of them told us the remedy for the future, which you have shared with us. For that, thank you very much, Pandit ji.

And if you listen to all the experiences of the devotees, you will see that nothing will happen to those who are in the stream of Dharma. All calamities will come, but a person who is in the stream will be saved. This has been proven, and it is proven time and again because this is the Lord's stream, not mine. God has given me some ability, and with His blessings, I am able to speak, tell you, and inform you about this. This is all God's blessing.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Why is it necessary to chant the name of the Lord specific to the current era (Yuga), like Madhav, instead of names from previous eras like Ram or Krishna?
Answer: Because according to the era, there is a mantra, a name, and an avatar of the Lord. For instance, in my previous life, my name was something different. In this life, if you call me by my current name, I will hear it. If you call me by my previous life's name, I will wonder who you are calling. The second point is that God takes an avatar in every yuga, and the name of that avatar is propagated according to that yuga. For example, in Satya Yuga, the name of Mahavishnu; in Treta Yuga, the name of Lord Sri Ram; in Dvapara Yuga, the name of Lord Sri Krishna; and in Kali Yuga, the name of Sri Jagannath. And in this transitional yuga where we stand, it is the name of Lord Kalki or Madhav. By chanting the name of the avatar according to the yuga, one receives God's blessings. Chanting Hare Krishna, Hare Ram is good for us and for the world. But for the calamity that is coming, the name of Madhav is necessary. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।

Nothing will happen to you, and nothing ever happens to the devotees. Therefore, focus on Dharma and purity.

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Pandit ji, you were saying that a yuga is not millions of years long, right? In 2018, you had explained the calculation: 1200, 2400, 3600, and 4800 years. I saw your video. This feels correct to me, as it seems the Ramayana era was not millions of years ago. If you could guide me on this, my doubt will be cleared.
Answer: Look, son, this Kali Yuga has come many times. For example, when Tulsidas ji wrote the Ramcharitmanas, 18 or 19 Kali Yugas had already passed. He has written this in the Ramcharitmanas. So, the Kali Yuga has come so many times that no one knows, because the universe is infinite. The yugas come and go continuously, people come and go, deities come, Dharma is established, sin increases, then destruction happens, and then the earth is purified. This cycle continues. If we consider all the scriptures, our knowledge will be limited. But the yuga cycle continues. In every yuga, devotees discuss these matters, just as we are discussing them today. No one knows how many yugas have passed or for how many years this will continue. But regarding the four yugas—Satya, Treta, Dvapara, Kali—the calculation is 1200, 2400, 3600, and 4800 years. As Dharma decreases, the duration of the yuga increases. In Satya Yuga, Dharma has four pillars. Then one pillar of Dharma is lost, and the duration doubles. Then another pillar is lost, and it triples. In Kali Yuga, only one pillar remains, and the duration quadruples. According to this calculation, Kali Yuga was meant to last for 4800 years. This is also written in the Manusmriti, the Garga Samhita, and the Nirnaya Sindhu, and the greatest divine boon is that it is also in the Malika. Okay?

Video Title: L34 जब बार-बार एक दिन में संक्रांति और अमावस्या एक साथ पड़ेगा, तब महाप्रलय आएगा।
Question: Why do all the pandits keep saying that the Kali Yuga is 432,000 years long? I just spoke to one, and he also said there is still a lot of time. Do they not study, or are they trapped in worldly illusion (moh-maya)?
Answer: Look, in this world, everyone is engaged in earning a livelihood. If they say the Kali Yuga is ending, then who will become their disciple, and how will they give blessings like, "You will have a family, children, you will build a house"? They get money by giving such blessings, so they deceive people. And they do not present the scriptures correctly before the people. If they don't know the scriptures, they call this knowledge anti-Vedic. I hear this from all over India. But a time will come when they themselves will start saying, "No, the Kali Yuga has ended." They will then change their tune. But we are not doing this work to show anyone in the world; we have no need for that. Our goal is that people take refuge, follow the Malika, practice righteousness, and, understanding that the yuga is ending, perform their duties so that human society can survive on earth and the earth's balance can be maintained. The light of Satya Dharma will emerge. What others do is none of our concern. We don't like to speak about anyone, but it is necessary for them to speak after reading the scriptures properly. If they don't read, they don't speak correctly. That is their mistake. God will be the judge of that.

Question: What will happen for human society in the new year, and what changes will come?
Answer: The coming year will be good for devotees and for those who follow the path of dharma, righteousness, ideals, and the Vedic system. For those who are in refuge with God and practice truth, peace, compassion, forgiveness, and friendship in their lives, there is nothing to worry about in the coming year. However, if human society does not improve and change its actions and conduct, it will face even more problems than in the previous year. Natural calamities will increase: there will be more fires, floods, and people will witness the effects of earthquakes and destruction. The disease that has been causing suffering since 2019 will become even more severe than it was in 2021, and new diseases will also emerge. There will be administrative chaos, social instability, a lack of peace, and rising communal violence. Problems and conflicts between the administration and the general public will increase. The younger generation will continue to face the same problems as before. There will be tensions between India and Pakistan. People will witness destruction in Asia, Europe, and Africa. Dormant volcanoes will become active. The biggest challenge for humanity will be poverty. Therefore, along with working hard to survive, it is most important for people to adopt the spiritual path of Sattva. I humbly request all of human society to hold onto dharma. You don't need to learn from what others in society are saying or doing. If there is something good in society, learn it; if there is evil, you have nothing to do with it. For those who want to stay in dharma, there will be no problem in 2022.

Question: Pandit ji, please tell me about Hanuman ji.
Answer: In the Jagannath Kshetra (Puri), the sea was repeatedly crossing its shore and roaring towards the temple. So, the Lord summoned Hanuman ji. Hanuman ji, who was in Ayodhya engaged in devotion to Lord Ram, came and said, 'Lord, I have come. I will stay here and ensure the sea does not cross its boundary.' He then requested the Lord to bind him so that he would not be able to leave. By Lord Jagannath's will, Hanuman ji is guarding the sea at the Jagannath temple. However, a time will come when Lord Jagannath leaves the Kshetra. At that time, Hanuman ji and all the other deities will also leave the area. The sea will then cross its shore, first over the 22 steps (Baisi Pahacha) and later, it will submerge even the Nila Chakra (the wheel at the top of the temple).

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE
Question: Pandit ji, I had a dream that I want to share with you. In the dream, I saw a young girl, about six years old, who comes holding a snake in her hand. My husband and I are standing there, and she says, 'This is Kalki ji.' My wife gets a little scared at first, but I say, 'If he is Kalki ji, there's no need to be afraid.' Then she gives the snake to me. It climbs onto my hand, kisses it, and goes away. Similarly, it then climbs onto my wife's head and also kisses her hand before leaving. So, Pandit ji, I had this dream. Could you please explain its meaning?
Answer: A very good question. Listen, everyone. The little girl who brought the snake in her hand is Mother Vaishno Devi, Mother Jogmaya herself. And the snake she was holding was Lord Kalki himself, because it is written in the Malika. In this age, Kalki's name is Satya Anant Madhav. Anant means Balaram, and Madhav means Kalki. In this era, Lord Kalki is the Anant power, meaning he will carry out destruction with the power of Balaram and nurture the devotees with the power of Lord Shri Krishna. In one body, there is both Anant and Lord Krishna. Lord Kalki's right side is Lord Balaram, and his left side is Lord Krishna. I will say this much: Lord Kalki will carry out the work of destruction in his Anant form, and one of his names is also Anant. So, you have received the blessings of Lord Kalki. You are fortunate. You must fully adopt righteousness, duty, and the spiritual path in your life. You have received the Lord's grace. This means that from now on, we will fully follow dharma and not commit any sin, unrighteousness, or injustice, and we will walk on the Lord's path. People who listen to the Malika have such dreams, and even those who don't will also have such dreams. They too will gradually come towards this path through the Malika. This propagation of the Malika is the path to the devotees' salvation. We are the medium, and you all are the Lord's council. You are fortunate, and you have received the grace of Anant, that is, Lord Vishnu.

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE
Question: I also want to share a dream. One day, I dreamt that extraterrestrial beings were coming to Earth in large numbers and taking away all the distressed people. Someone was saying that a great deluge is coming in a few days, so everyone should leave. I was repeatedly pleading with them, saying, 'If the deluge is coming, we will die right here, on our motherland. Why are you forcing us? Whoever wants to go of their own free will can go. Don't take those who don't want to leave.' But they were still taking everyone away. What does this mean, Pandit ji?
Answer: Look, what is destined to happen will surely happen. But see, when the great flood occurs, the Lord will act through his Jogmaya power. He will save the devotees in some form, in some body, through some medium. This is written in the Malika. So, only the devotees will be saved; the unrighteous will not be rescued. This is going to happen. As for extraterrestrial beings, look, we are Hindus. It is written in our scriptures that we live in the mortal realm (Martya Lok), which is inhabited by humans. Apart from the mortal realm, there are the Moon realm (Chandra Lok), the Sun realm (Surya Lok), the realm of gods (Dev Lok), heaven (Swarg Lok), and Golok, Vaikunth, and many such places where gods, yakshas, rakshasas, gandharvas, sages, and seers reside. They generally do not harm anyone or cause sorrow. They stay in their own realm, and humans stay in theirs. When the Lord gives the command, they will come and perform their duties. Okay, baba?

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE
Question: Pandit ji, I love the Lord very much. But in my family, they don't give me vegetarian food every day. After eating tamasic (non-vegetarian) food, I feel very hesitant to chant the Lord's name. They say, 'Look, your body needs protein every day, and we can't provide milk, ghee, and butter daily.' What should I do? I find it very difficult to chant the Lord's name; my mind feels disturbed. I can't chant; I feel very hesitant in my heart.
Answer: You should humbly request your family, your parents. Who is the most powerful in this world? What does a horse eat? Does a horse eat fish, non-veg, meat? No. So why do scientists and society consider the horse to be so powerful? It eats grass. So it is a vegetarian. It also gets strength. The one who eats meat and fish incurs sin, and in exchange for the strength they get, diseases also spread in their body. A time will come, and it has already started, where cancer and many other diseases have spread among animals. It is written in the Malika that first, a large number of fish and animals will die from diseases, and then the same diseases will come to human society. So, this means that the one who has the greatest purity is the most powerful. Tell your parents, 'Look, a horse eats so little, an elephant doesn't eat meat, yet they are so strong. Where does that power come from?' The meaning of Ayurveda is that by directly consuming roots, herbs, and plants, the body is nourished, and there are no chemicals. The chicken or goat, what do they eat? They eat plants and grass. So what is the problem if we directly eat fruits, flowers, and vegetarian food?

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE

Question: Pandit ji, what can be offered as bhog (sacred food offering)? What kind of bhog should be offered?
Answer: Satvik food, which is made with ghee, or even oil, that's fine. And what is made from vegetarian ingredients and fruits, that is all considered Satvik. That is the food that is offered as bhog in temples. Then there is Rajasik food, which includes things like onions, garlic, and spicy food, considered fit for kings and emperors. It is not offered in temples. And Tamasik food includes meat, fish, and alcohol.

Video Title: L43 कलियुग अंत होने के 13 प्रमुख कारण, 13 main reasons for the end of Kali Yuga,-- LIVE
Question: I am a devotee of Lord Vishnu since childhood. I am not able to do long prayers because of my office work, but I manage to do Tri-sandhya (prayers at three junctures of the day) and read the Bhagwat on my days off. Is this enough, or should I be doing more?
Answer: Our first office is our body and liberation. The second office is our worldly life where we earn a living. If you don't have time during the day, then at night before sleeping, purify yourself and read the Shrimad Bhagwat for 10 minutes, mother. Because the Bhagwat is the life-giving nectar. The Bhagwat is the first step on the path of Satya Yuga. The Bhagwat itself will save the devotees. So at the very least, mother, if not during the day, then at night before sleeping, read the Bhagwat for 10 minutes. And in the Tri-sandhya, chant the 16 names, the 10 avatars, the Vasudev mantra, and a little Durga-Madhav stuti. This will take 5 to 10 minutes. 10 minutes three times a day is 30 minutes. Reading the Shrimad Bhagwat will protect you from the diseases and problems that are coming to the world. It will become a protective shield for you.

Question: Why does the Lord have to take a human birth in every age?
Answer: The Shrimad Ramcharitmanas says, 'Whenever righteousness declines and demons and the arrogant rise, the Lord takes a human form.' The scriptures say that whenever there is a decline in Dharma (righteousness), whether in the Dvapara or Kali Yuga, the Lord assumes a human form. He comes in a human body to transform people. When the Lord comes in a human form, the people and devotees on Earth can connect with Him. In a way, the Lord's divine play (leela) happens with the devotees. If the Lord were to come in His divine form to establish Dharma in every age, then all the sinners in the world would take refuge in Him out of fear, and the establishment of Dharma wouldn't be necessary. The Lord would see that everyone has surrendered to Him, so how would He distinguish between the virtuous and the sinful? That is why the Lord comes in secret. For example, the Lord was born in Kansa's prison, and Lord Rama was born in Ayodhya, went to the forest, and destroyed the demons in secret. When Lord Rama came to establish Dharma, many kings in India were not ready to accept him as God. He doesn't come in a divine form but in a human body with human characteristics. He engages in human-like divine play (manav leela) with people and devotees and carries out his mission of struggle.

Question: Narada, what was your previous birth, and how did devotion to God and divine inspiration awaken in your mind? How did this happen?
Answer: Narada Mahamuni explained this in detail in the Srimad Bhagavatam. He said: In my previous life, my mother was a maidservant to some great people, and I was her only son. My mother raised me with great love and purity, always keeping me in her sight. She served in the king's house day and night, and I grew up there. However, due to the circumstances of time, I didn't mix with other people. I lived a simple life and had no interest in making friends or playing like other small children. As time passed, when I was a five-year-old boy, I got an opportunity for spiritual association (satsang). At the place where my mother worked, some saints and Brahmins came to stay for 'Chaturmasya' (the four months of the rainy season), as they would stop their travels and stay in one place. The owner of the house where my mother worked appointed me to serve them. My mother was also serving these saints and Brahmins, and I too got engaged in their service. While serving the Brahmins, by observing their worship, prayers, hymns, and devotion to the Bhagavatam, and by sitting with them and participating in their activities, devotion and the light of knowledge for God awakened within me. I stayed with them and would eat only once a day, and that too, I would eat the leftovers from the Brahmins' plates. All the Brahmins and holy men loved me very much and blessed me. Gradually, they explained the essence of the Bhagavatam to me. Through their teachings, I became immersed in the love of God and the Bhagavatam. When the four months of Chaturmasya ended, the Brahmins blessed me, saying, 'May the light of pure devotion to God shine in your heart, may you take refuge in God, and may His grace always be upon you.' After they left, some days passed. One night, my mother went out to milk the cows and was bitten by a snake, and she passed away. I had no one else; my father had already passed away, and I had no other family. Through the saints, the desire to attain God had already grown strong in my heart, and worldly attachments had gradually faded. After my mother's death, I left that place and started walking towards the north. On my way, I passed many huts and towns, but my mind did not settle anywhere. I continued walking and entered a dense forest, which had many ferocious animals. But my mind was fixed on devotion to God, so I paid no attention to the dangers. Then, near a reservoir, I felt tired and sat down. After drinking some water and bathing, my fatigue went away. I decided to sit in meditation, just as the Brahmins and saints had taught me, that it is easier to receive God's grace by sitting in a solitary, secluded place. When I found such a place and environment, I sat down and began to meditate on the infinite form of the Lord that the Brahmin saints had described. Slowly, I delved deeper into devotion. After some time passed in this state, I had a vision of the Lord's form. I saw the four-armed Narayana. It was so blissful that all worldly desires in my mind were destroyed; I could only see God everywhere. After a few moments, the Lord disappeared. I felt sad, but I did not give up. I sat in the same place and continued my penance and meditation on God. As I meditated, the Lord understood my sorrow and spoke to me through a divine voice from the sky, saying, 'You will not attain me in this birth. However, because of your devotion and spiritual impressions, I gave you this momentary vision. You will attain me in the next age.' He said that after the cosmic dissolution (pralaya), His memory would remain in my mind, and I would continue to worship Him and be in His refuge. Time passed, and I continued my penance, immersed in divine love. At the time of the great dissolution, when Lord Brahma was creating, I was born from Brahma's breath (mind) as his son, Narada. As was the Lord's blessing, I was always engaged in chanting the Lord's name and in divine knowledge. I received the power to travel anywhere in heaven, earth, or the netherworld without any obstruction. Thus, I became the mind-born son of Brahma.

Question: In our tradition, we sing the Durga Madhava Stuti (hymn). It says that Mahamaya (the great illusion/Goddess Durga) is omnipresent in the form of Madan Mohan (a name for Krishna). How is this possible? Please shed some light on this.
Answer: What is written there is a union of both Durga and Madhava. In one place, Mother Durga is praised with names like Rama, Uma, Vani, Radha, and in another place, Durga and Madhava are venerated together. The line 'omnipresent in the form of Madan Mohan' does not refer to Mother Durga. It is a praise of Madhava (Krishna). The hymn venerates both; one part describes Madhava, which you are referring to as Madan Mohan, and the other part, 'Durga Rama Vani Radha,' praises Mother Durga. So, both are venerated in the hymn.

Question: In the Satya Yuga, which incarnation of Lord Narayana was there?
Answer: In the Satya Yuga, it was the incarnation of Lord Vishnu. In the Satya Yuga, the Lord took four main avatars to establish dharma: Matsya (the fish), Kurma (the tortoise), Varaha (the boar), and Narasimha (the man-lion), and the Vamana (dwarf) form is also included. These forms were manifested when needed. For instance, when the Vedas needed to be rescued, the Lord took the Matsya avatar to save them and spread their light in the world. The Kurma avatar was for the churning of the ocean, from which gods, yakshas, gandharvas, humans, medicinal herbs, and all elements essential for the universe were created. In the Varaha avatar, the Lord established the Earth in its proper place in the cosmos. In the Narasimha avatar, the Lord saved his devotee Prahlada and vanquished the demon Hiranyakashipu. In the Vamana avatar, he subdued King Bali and sent him to the netherworld. So, avatars appear in every age. These were the special avatars of the Lord in the Satya Yuga. But above all, Lord Vishnu is the primary deity of the Satya Yuga. The scriptures say, 'Krite yad dhyayato Vishnum,' meaning in the Krita Yuga (Satya Yuga), everyone meditates on Lord Vishnu.
