Therefore, if a father, mother, or anyone in the family passes away, their ashes should be immersed as soon as possible, even after two or three months, by bathing in the Ganga. After that, you can go on all pilgrimages, observe vows, and perform worship. However, auspicious ceremonies like weddings should happen after one year. House construction should also happen after one year. But reading the Bhagavat can be done even after the 12th day following the death. There is no issue. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L172: पश्चिम दिशा में सूर्य उदित होंगे वो भी मध्य रात्रि में | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: My question is, the Lord does so much for us. What is our duty towards the Lord? Please tell us a little about that.
Answer: Look, I want to slightly change the language of the idea of 'our duty towards the Lord.' What is the duty of a human life? The Lord is our father; He gives us everything. We have nothing to offer in gratitude. There is just one thing: what is our duty towards ourselves? A human's first duty towards themself is to perform virtuous deeds. The second duty is to speak the truth. And the third duty is to earn a living through hard work. A man who does these three things is a true man, and this human life finds its meaning. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L172: पश्चिम दिशा में सूर्य उदित होंगे वो भी मध्य रात्रि में | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Today is the first day I am speaking with you. Pandit Ji, who are you? You don't seem like an ordinary person to me. Please answer this. If you cannot answer in front of everyone, that's okay, but this was a question from my heart. And secondly, what will happen during the Pisces-Saturn transit? Please provide guidance on that.
Answer: Look, regarding the question you asked about me, I was born into a Brahmin family. My name is Pandit Kashinath Mishra, and I am doing the work of propagating dharma. This is my duty in God's account. That is all about me. And your second question, what will happen during the Pisces-Saturn conjunction? This Pisces-Saturn transit that is happening, in which the nine planets and two other entities, Saturn's Gulika and Mandi, also have an obstructive aspect on the Earth. During this time, broadly speaking, Jupiter is becoming very powerful, and Saturn is supporting it. Mars is somewhat troublesome for the Earth, and Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto have an obstructive aspect on the Earth. Due to this, there will be situations like floods, fire cataclysms, land disasters, atomic war, and earthquakes on Earth. The government and administrative system will move towards dharma, meaning it will transform into a spiritual governance system. This will take 10 years, not just a few days, it will take 8-10 years. And during the Pisces-Saturn transit, dharma will rise. This means that the lifespan of those who live by dharma will increase, their mental strength will increase, and they will also receive God's grace and His company. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L173: WW3 | तीसरे विश्व युद्ध का परिणाम क्या होगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: You had mentioned the method for a seven-day Shrimad Bhagwat Katha. What is the complete procedure, rules, and the auspicious time to begin it?
Answer: Look, the world's most auspicious and holy scripture is the one that destroys the three types of miseries, from which man gets liberation from the cycle of suffering. 'Tapa-traya vinashaya Sri Krishnaya vayam namah' (We bow to Lord Krishna, the destroyer of the three miseries). It is always an auspicious scripture. If someone wants to conduct a seven-day Bhagwat Katha at their home, I give this advice: gather all the family members and complete the reading of this scripture in seven days, either from sunrise to sunset, or 'ahoratra' (day and night), meaning from one sunrise to the next. If you have fewer family members, you can also invite people from the Madhav family who read the Bhagwat. For instance, there are twelve cantos (skandhas). You can assign one canto to each person, or divide the cantos among them—like two, one and a half, half—so that by sitting together, the complete reading of the Bhagwat can be finished in seven days. For this, you will need to perform a 'kalash sthapana' (installing a sacred pot) and invoke Lord Ganapati. On the day you begin the Bhagwat Katha, after invoking Ganapati, you must feed a cow—give her roti and whatever fruits you can. This should be done every day for seven days. During these seven days of the Bhagwat Katha, no one in the house should sleep on a bed; they should sleep on a mat, not on a mattress. A simple life must be maintained. Husband and wife should stay apart. There should be no laughter or joking around. During this period, there should be no loud talking, anger, or arguments in the house. If you read the Bhagwat day and night for seven days like this, then along with your ancestors, all your problems will be resolved, in my opinion. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L173: WW3 | तीसरे विश्व युद्ध का परिणाम क्या होगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: We are currently living in this society and have found a resale flat that we like. Our current flat is A204, while the new flat is 1337. Would it be appropriate to buy this flat? Please guide us.
Answer: Look, 1337... 13... 37... See, the Kumbh also started on the 13th. So this 1337, 13, 37 is a righteous number. It will be good, there is no harm. You can live there happily. But let me say one thing... if I speak about what people are doing nowadays, it will affect their business, so I will discuss that separately. But there is nothing inauspicious about taking that house; it is good. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L173: WW3 | तीसरे विश्व युद्ध का परिणाम क्या होगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: We try to help as many people as we can in our daily lives, and we do. But when we need help, almost 99% of people do not help us. Why does this happen? (The questioner adds that when no one helps until the very end, God helps through some means or another).
Answer: Look, this is the age of Kali Yuga. In this era, the light of Dharma (righteousness) is dim, and its influence is also diminishing. That is why these things are reflected. But a time will come in the future when everyone will help in every task. For instance, if we have grain in our house and others don't, everyone will say, 'There is so much grain, let's all eat together as a village.' Such a time will also come. So, time is moving towards change, and it will take a little while. Because of Kali Yuga... look at Shrimad Mahabharata, Shrimad Bhagwat, Shrimad Gita, Narada Purana, all the Puranas—all of them have written about the greatness of Trikal Sandhya, which was not being propagated until now. I thank the devotees of the Vishwa Sanatan Dharma Seva Trust who propagated Trikal Sandhya to lakhs of people at the Kumbh Mela. Because if there is any greater principle in the world—no matter how knowledgeable one is, how well-versed in the scriptures—come, I will show you about Trikal Sandhya. There is no greater power, no greater Dharma, no greater action, no greater achievement in human life than this. Therefore, O human, understand your situation. Understand the control and state of your life's flow, the purpose of your birth. The fulfillment of this life is found in Trikal Sandhya and nowhere else. I have read all the scriptures, and there is no principle greater than this. I invite anyone who wants to deliberate on this to come and have a discussion with me. So, you should perform Trikal Sandhya. When calamity strikes, even your wife will say, 'I will also do it. Why am I coughing? Why do I have a cold? Why am I getting a virus? I will also perform Trikal Sandhya.' Just wait for that time. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L173: WW3 | तीसरे विश्व युद्ध का परिणाम क्या होगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: How will we know which devotees will survive and enter Satyug (the Golden Age)? Is this mentioned in the Bhavishya Malika? How will we know if we will be the ones to survive?
Answer: Look, it is written in the Malika scripture that those who practice truth, peace, compassion, and forgiveness, and perform 'Brahma Karma' will survive. What is Brahma Karma? Trikal Sandhya, which the sages and saints used to perform. Understand this well. In Satyug, Tretayug, and Dwaparyug, the sages, perfected beings, and saints were performing Brahma Karma. And for Kali Yuga, it is categorically written that those who perform Brahma Karma will have a vision of the Absolute (Brahman), will attain God, and will go to Satyug. So, there is no principle other than Trikal Sandhya. Those who perform it—not according to my ideology, but according to the ideology of the scriptures—will become worthy of Satyug.

Video Title: L173: WW3 | तीसरे विश्व युद्ध का परिणाम क्या होगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Has Lord Kalki taken birth in this Kali Yuga as a female or as a male?
Answer: Ah, that is a very good question. But I want to say this much: if Lord Kalki has taken birth and the questioner knows about it, then by seeing him, they can surely get an idea. But since they have asked, I respect their question and want to state that God is neither female nor male. He is not a human, nor a deity, nor a Yaksha or Gandharva, nor a sage. God is formless, without attributes, yet with attributes. We call him Akaar Vishnu or Nirakaar Vishnu (Formless Vishnu). Sometimes, God took the form of Mohini to distribute the nectar of immortality. At other times, the Lord took the universal form (Vishwaroop) for the creation of the earth. Sometimes, the Lord took the form of Narasimha Mahaprabhu to destroy the demons. So, God is 'Arupavanta', meaning we cannot conceive of His form. He is God, He is our Lord, He is our Supreme Father. To deliberate on His form in terms of male or female is perhaps beyond our human capacity. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Rajat Prajapati from Varanasi has asked: If it is not possible to bathe during Brahma Muhurta (the auspicious time before sunrise), how can one perform Tri-Sandhya (prayers at three junctures of the day)?
Answer: Look, no one becomes a Brahma Rishi (a sage of the highest order) in a single day. No one becomes a sage or a seer in a day, nor does one become knowledgeable in a day. So, first, make it a habit. This means, after waking up from your bed, you can perform Tri-Sandhya by chanting the name of Mother Ganga Devi three times in your mind. And when the era changes, the devotees will also become strong and powerful. They too will wake up during Brahma Muhurta, bathe, and then perform it. But first, make it a habit. You can perform Tri-Sandhya by chanting the name of Mother Ganga. Let me tell you one thing: when Lord Krishna was born in the prison in Mathura, at that time, Vasudev ji did not bathe. But he chanted the name of Mother Ganga and took an oath to donate a thousand cows for the birth and welfare of the Lord. So, anything is possible when the mind is pure, and nothing is possible when the mind is impure. Therefore, you can wake up during Brahma Muhurta and do it. Whether you bathe or not, you can do it by chanting the name of Mother Ganga. Jai.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: The second question is from Rahul Kumar in Delhi. In a job or place where alcohol, meat, indecent language, and wrong behavior are prevalent, should one quit that job or look for another job elsewhere? Please guide us, Pandit ji.
Answer: My respectful greetings to you too. Tulsidas ji wrote in a couplet in the Ramcharitmanas, 'A saint is like a swan who separates milk from water, accepting the good and rejecting the bad.' He said this world, both animate and inanimate, is full of virtues and vices. So, in every environment, there is impurity. What is this impurity? It is adopting the elements of the Kali Yuga: consuming alcohol, speaking lies, harming others, killing living beings, and doing wrong deeds. These places where Kali (the personification of the dark age) resides are numerous on this earth. So, Tulsidas ji said the world is full of flaws, but you have to do one thing. Just as a swan leaves the water and drinks the milk, we should do the same. It is not our duty to watch what others are doing or not doing. We should stay away from such company. Where you have your duty, where you are employed, do your job and pray to God, 'Lord, save me from this environment.' And when God gives you another job somewhere else, then join that job. And just as a swan imbibes good qualities, similarly, among those who do wrong, look for the good qualities in them. Forget the bad, don't look at the bad, don't think about the bad. And in time, pray to God, 'Lord, save me from this environment,' because the environment makes a person bad. And I pray for you that you will be saved. In my opinion, you will be delivered. Jai.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Has the Kali Yuga (the current age of vice) occurred before? And is the current one the 28th Kali Yuga? Did the previous Kali Yugas last for more than 5000 years or not? Please clear my confusion.
Answer: Regarding the age of the previous Kali Yuga, look, it is not very clear in the scriptures. However, it is written in the Brahma Purana that in the previous Kali Yuga—that is, in the cycle of Satya, Treta, Dwapara, Kali that has passed before this one—at that time, Lord Kalki was born with the name Pramit Chandra. At the age of 30, he established dharma for 22 years. And for those 22 years, he established dharma sometimes riding a horse, sometimes an elephant. At that time, the Lord was also married. And the Lord first killed those who committed the most sins, then those who committed half the sins, and after that, those who committed minor sins. At that time, he traveled through China and countries of different religions and faiths, roaming the entire universe for about 20-22 years, establishing dharma and destroying all sinners. So, Kali Yuga comes in every yuga cycle, and the age of Kali Yuga is similar in every yuga. Because, look, the amount of water in the ocean today was the same in the Dwapara Yuga, the same in the Treta Yuga, and the same in the Satya Yuga. There were seven seas, and even today there are seven seas, not eight. So, just as the volume of water is fixed, the volume of sin is also similar in every yuga. Whether you talk about this yuga or the previous one, it runs on the same principle, and the Lord balances it. Jai.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: The next question is from Suraj Kumar Jain from Odisha. Can we read the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana even after 12 AM? That is, can we read it all night?
Answer: Look, there is no fixed time for worshipping God. Because some are sick, some are grieving, some are working, and some are engaged in ordinary tasks. Reading in the middle of the night is not forbidden. But God also sleeps for some time, right? For example, it is written in our scriptures that Lord Jagannath leaves the Jagannath Kshetra (holy land) in the middle of the night and goes to Shri Vrindavan, where he performs the eternal Raas Leela (divine dance). So, it is not right to disturb God at that time. But the thing is, where there is an 'Ahoratra Katha' (day and night narration), 'Ahoratra' reading takes place. 'Ahoratra' means from sunrise to sunset, from sunset to 12 AM, and then from 12 AM to sunrise. If the narration is for seven days like the one King Parikshit heard, with that kind of vow, then one should not look at the time. If our seven-day narration is not under that vow, then I would say read from sunrise until 12 AM. Do not read after 12. You can start again from Brahma Muhurta. Jai.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: This question is from Sunil Singh from Chhapra, Bihar. What information is given in the Malika regarding the planet Mercury? What are the details about Madhav Mahaprabhu's leela (divine play) in Mecca-Medina? Will both these events happen during the Meen-Shani yog (Pisces-Saturn conjunction)?
Answer: A very good and important question. About the planet Mercury, it is written that beings residing there—whether they are like humans or gods, whatever they may be, some people call them aliens—no one knows or recognizes them. But it is written in the Malika scripture that inhabitants from the planet Mercury will come, and under the Lord's command, they will create a dance of destruction with rain all over the world, which will create a very terrible situation for sinners, and there will be a great flood. This is written. The second question is about Madhav Mahaprabhu's leela in Mecca-Medina and if both events will happen during the Meen-Shani yog. Look, it is written that when the sea god reaches the 22 steps of the Jagannath temple, at that same time, there will be a great, fierce battle in the holy region of Mecca-Medina. It is also written that the sea will come from the east, and fish will play on the 22 steps. The sea god Varuna will sit on the jeweled throne, and Lord Jagannath will reside at Chhatia Dham. And when Lord Jagannath leaves the Jagannath Kshetra, the sea will follow him. This is written. It will be a direct manifestation because the sea god Varuna will follow Jagannath ji, just as when Lord Krishna left, Dwarka was submerged in water. Similarly, after Jagannath ji leaves, the Dwarka of Kali Yuga, or what is called Vaikuntha, will be covered by the sea. This is written. So, at the time the sea reaches the 22 steps, there will be a battle in Mecca-Medina. Now, whether this will happen during the Meen-Shani transit or after it, is the Lord's decision. I have no opinion on this. That's why I have repeatedly said in the Malika scripture that whatever the Lord wishes, He will do. It will happen for sure, there is no question about it. And the time is near, meaning it will happen in the future. It is subject to the Lord's will. At what time the Lord will do it, I am not worthy to comment on. But the idea is that it is 1,000,000% true, and as time goes on, while saving the devotees, the Lord will do it at the right time, in a way that the devotees will not suffer and dharma will be protected.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Indrajit Singh from Vadodara, Gujarat, asks: It is written in the 'Nau Khand Nirghant'. Please explain its meaning. 'Know the bondage of the three gunas in the one-syllable seed of love, the Shodashi. Remember Hare Ram Krishna. Turn it upside down and look. Ask Biraja again.'
Answer: Look, Achyutananda ji had said one thing, let me tell you that first. Many Malika texts are written in a coded language. Mahapurush Achyutananda wrote them with hints, and he also explains them. Achyutananda ji wrote one thing about this: 'I have explained the secret words in truth. I will explain the coded language I created in Satya Yuga; no human will be able to understand it. And when you are born, the scriptures I will reveal will be like lines etched in stone.' But I will tell you an essential truth. The one-syllable mantra at this time is the name 'Madhava'. It is written about this: 'With eight arms and eight weapons, at the end of Kali, Madhava will be born. Whoever chants the name Madhava will be liberated from birth and death.' This one-syllable mantra saves humanity and helps attain Vaikuntha. And among the Pancha Sakha, the holy Balaram Das Maharaj has written that those who chant the name Madhava will be freed from the bondage of birth and death and will attain Vaikuntha. So, if we consider the one-syllable mantra, the three gunas, and this element, it is the name 'Madhava'. And when it comes to chanting the Mahamantra in reverse, let me tell you one thing. Everyone chants the Mahamantra, but in Tri-Sandhya, the mantra is 'Hare Ram Hare Ram Ram Ram Hare Hare, Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare'. But Mother Biraja Devi will ask for the reverse mantra. What is the reverse Mahamantra? At that time, the devotees will say: 'Ram Hare Krishna Hare, Hum Hare Krishna, Hare Ram Hare Krishna, Hare Ram Hare Krishna, Hare Ram Hare Krishna, Hare Ananta Madhava, Hare Ram Hare Krishna, Hare Ram Hare Krishna Hare.' The devotees will tell this mantra to Mother Biraja Devi, and she will let them pass. This mantra is very powerful, and it is propagated only in our tradition; it is not propagated anywhere else. And the value of this mantra will be understood when Yogamaya Mother Biraja Devi tests them. So, devotees should chant this mantra as well as the Mahamantra. Chant this mantra a little during Tri-Sandhya. It is very blissful and very powerful in the Kali Yuga. Jai.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: But I want to save some of my friends, so how can I save them?
Answer: This is a very joyful thing because we have not come for ourselves. He is asking from Odisha and saying, 'I am giving up non-veg, I am doing Tri-Sandhya, and I have attended three Google meetings.' Look, it is my good fortune to interact with devotees with such a pure heart as yours. Because this life is for salvation. Just as I told you, you also want salvation for others. This devotee wants to save his companions. So, I give one piece of advice: don't say anything to them directly. Just say, 'Brother, do one thing, a big change will come in your life.' 'What should I do?' 'Chant the name of Madhava.' Chant it in your mind, sometimes on the road, in the village, at home, at work, while doing any task, or while sleeping. And read the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam. If they ask, 'What will we get?' just tell them, 'You will get a job, you will get peace and happiness, there will be peace at home, ancestral curses will be removed, and the family members who listen to this will also become pure. And most importantly, this life will be saved, and the problems we are struggling with will go away.' First, if they read the tenth canto of the Bhagavatam for some time, for a few days, and chant the name of Madhava, in my opinion, they will also start doing Tri-Sandhya, they will give up meat and fish, they will give up everything and become saints. Saints are not made by their attire but by their actions. A person with a job can be a saint, a businessman can be a saint, and someone who has left their family can also be a saint. So, the actions must be good. You will have to bring about these small changes in them. They won't understand all at once. They will change if you tell them. If you say, 'It will be for your own benefit,' because people in Kali Yuga want to know, 'What's in it for me?' If among them there is a devotee who is destined for Satya Yuga, in my opinion, they will do it. But if it is not in their destiny to go to Satya Yuga, then even if we tell them a million times, there will be no change. Jai Jagannath. Thank you.

Video Title: L174: Bhavishya Malika Volume 2 | भविष्य मालिका पुराण पार्ट 2 कब आएगा? | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Pandit ji, can we attain moksha (liberation) by performing Tri-Sandhya and reading the Bhagavatam?
Answer: Look, in the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana, the Lord told Uddhava regarding Sandhya. He said that those who do not perform Tri-Sandhya are living corpses. That is, despite being born and mobile, they are like a dead body. And those who do not perform Tri-Sandhya have no direction in their life. Those who do perform Tri-Sandhya get freedom from the cycle of three sins, and people who perform Tri-Sandhya attain Vaikuntha (the celestial abode). The Lord says this. It's not just in the Bhagavata Mahapurana; it is written in the Bhagavata Purana, the Mahabharata, the Vishnu Purana, the Vayu Purana, the Brahma Purana, the Kurma Purana—it is written in many scriptures. The one stream of liberation is Tri-Sandhya. Whoever performs Tri-Sandhya is guaranteed to attain Vaikuntha; there is no change in this. The scriptures say this because what is written in the scriptures shows us the way. By following it, we have seen sages, perfected saints like Narada Mahamuni, who went from being human to becoming incarnations of God. Dhruva, from a human—or rather, from a demon clan, Dhruva and Prahlada—attained the position of Indra and became kings in Vaikuntha. So, human life is the precursor to becoming a deity, and Tri-Sandhya is a holy nectar for salvation. In it lies the salvation of humanity. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L175: धर्म एकमात्र रास्ता है | अभी भी समय है मानव सभ्यता के लिए | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Why is the Bhavishya Malika being propagated?
Answer: Today, why do we contemplate the scriptures and Puranas? It is because the Earth is heading towards a time of transformation. And at the time of this epochal change, the greatest power in human civilization will be Dharma (righteousness). A time will come when all of humanity will be united under one system, one thought, one sacred stream. Due to the severe Kali Yuga, there is no humanity left in human civilization today. Everyone perceives themselves differently; the world is watching. Therefore, the Bhavishya Malika scripture gives a message of truth, peace, compassion, forgiveness, and friendship to the entire universe. We will have to follow the path of truth. Without truth, peace, compassion, forgiveness, and friendship, it is impossible for any human to live on Earth. That's why we have to change. It is written in the Bhavishya Malika that a time will come when everyone will be united, everyone will come together. The whole world is moving towards a time of becoming one family of God, our Sanatani concept of 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (the world is one family). That is why Achyutananda ji said, 'Renounce illusion, attachment, and arrogance from your mind. Those who do not will face destruction by the Divine Mother.' So, this is the ideology of today, and this is why we propagate the Malika: so that every human helps every other human, brotherhood develops among all, and compassion for all living beings arises in every person.

Video Title: L175: धर्म एकमात्र रास्ता है | अभी भी समय है मानव सभ्यता के लिए | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: Why was this called a secret matter?
Answer: Because for about 4,600 years of this Kali Yuga's age of around 5,000 years, this was a secret. In Kali Yuga, no one knew about Satya Yuga (the Golden Age). Yes, the Kalki avatar is mentioned in the Bhagavata Mahapurana. It is also written in the Vishnu Purana and the Kalki Purana. Lord Vedavyasa has described it in many scriptures he wrote, but the exact timing was not determined. No scripture specified the exact period or time when this Kali Yuga would end. This information is found in only one scripture, and that scripture is the Bhavishya Malika.

Video Title: L175: धर्म एकमात्र रास्ता है | अभी भी समय है मानव सभ्यता के लिए | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: What is the goal of Bhavishya Malika?
Answer: The goal is this: O human, it is necessary for you to follow the rules of our Sanatana Dharma. And what are the rules of Sanatana Dharma? So, in my view, non-vegetarians can perform Trikal Sandhya. For those who eat non-vegetarian food but seek protection, along with Trikal Sandhya, they should also read the Bhagavata. In my opinion, after just a few months or days, you will say, 'No, I will not eat non-vegetarian food.' If you try to eat it, you might feel like vomiting, and your body will reject it. So, yes, you can do it. Jai!

Video Title: L176: बस ! 48 घंटे और फिर ... | मीन - शनि संयोग | 48 Hours later | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र
Question: If Bhavishya Malika is the final scripture of Hinduism, then why don't more people know about it? And in this Trikal Sandhya, why isn't the Gayatri Mantra given as much importance, and why was the name 'Madhav' chosen as the single-syllable mantra?
Answer: The Bhavishya Malika is a secret doctrine, written for devotees. It is a hidden truth meant only for the salvation of devotees. Those who are devotees follow it. Those who are not, no matter how much we propagate it—and we do propagate it all over India—they don't believe in it or follow it. That is why Achyutananda wrote that this doctrine is only for the devotees who have taken birth before, and only they will follow and believe in the Bhavishya Malika. Those who are not devotees will not believe. This is the reason. And why is the name 'Madhav' the single-syllable mantra? Because Madhav is Lord Mahavishnu. 'Ma' is the form of Brahma, 'Dha' is Prakriti (nature), the divine Narayani, famously known as Vishnu's eternal Maya. Radha is Yogamaya personified, Vishnu's eternal Maya. Radha is Vasundhara (the Earth), and with her is her lord, Madhav. This means Radha, Krishna, and Yogamaya all combine to form the name Madhav. And this very name, Madhav, is the single-syllable mantra. Balaram Das ji, one of the Panchasakha, has clearly stated in his scripture that at the end of Kali Yuga, Mahavishnu will be born with the name Madhav, and those who chant the name Madhav will be liberated from the cycle of birth and death. Jai!

Question: What topics have you shed light on in the second volume of the Bhavishya Malika Purana?

Question: Did the Ramayana and Mahabharata occur in the Chaturyuga of the same Manu, or in the Chaturyugas of different Manus within this Kalpa?
Answer: Look, in the cycle of yugas, Lord Ram comes, Lord Krishna comes, and the Mahabharata also happens. According to the Vayu Purana, there is clear evidence that even before that, Kali Yuga had come to Earth, at which time Lord Kalki's name was Pranit Chandra, or something similar. Therefore, from the very beginning of the ages, when the Lord assumed his cosmic form (Virat Roop), Lord Brahma was created from him, and Lord Vishnu recited the Bhagavat to Lord Brahma. The Mahabharata is also in the Bhagavat Purana. The Krishna Leela is also in the Bhagavat Purana. The essence of the eighteen Puranas is in the Srimad Bhagavat Mahapurana. From this, we get proof. In every age, gods, sages, and saints come and reconstruct the scriptures. They rewrite them. All are the words from the mouth of Lord Vishnu. And in the beginning age, the Kumar Sarga, all scriptures were composed because the Sanakadi Kumaras told Narada Mahamuni about reading the Bhagavat for seven days, as they originated in the Adi Sarga and had knowledge about all this. So, according to the yuga, the Lord's incarnations happen; there are 24 primary incarnations and countless others, and these scriptures are also revived, sometimes by Lord Vyasa Dev and sometimes by other accomplished saints. This is a continuous process, a tradition that goes on.

Question: Amulya Bhai from Karnataka asks: You said that a devotee will know themselves through 'Brahma Karma'. What exactly is Brahma Karma? The Bhagavad Gita says that Brahma Karma means to know oneself. Is knowing oneself enough?
Answer: Look, first I will tell you about Brahma Karma. 'Brahma' means Lord Vishnu. The method of worship and adoration of Lord Vishnu is called Brahma Karma. That is, the worship method of Lord Vishnu. Lord Vishnu is formless and also has a form. Lord Vishnu is also without shape. He is with attributes and without attributes. He is manifest and unmanifest. It is His will. 'Nija ichha nimit tanudhari' means He assumes a body by His own will. So, Brahma Karma is this Trikal Sandhya, this reading of the Bhagavat. By doing this, a person comes to know their own state. 'State' means, why were we born? What is the goal of our life? For what reason have we come to this earth, and what actions must we perform? How can we be liberated from the bondage of this life cycle, and after liberation, what is the ultimate purpose and highest state of human life? A person learns about all this when they perform Trikal Sandhya. Therefore, through the medium of Brahma Karma, through the Bhagavat, a person gets information about their state—that is, their limits, creation, and end. Then, accordingly, the person behaves and transforms their social and spiritual actions, moving towards liberation and the attainment of the main goal. This is called self-realization, and the person knows themselves—why they got this body, where to go, where to come from, everything becomes clear. So, reflect on this. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L178: जगन्नाथ क्षेत्र से धर्म संस्थापना का एक और संकेत! | पंडित काशीनाथ मिश्र

Therefore, if your parents forbid you, respect them completely. First, respect your parents. After that, you can discuss it with me. Because I also respect parents. They are the creators. They have given us birth. It's okay if they don't believe. Their life's karmic path is controlled by their past life's 'prarabdha' (destiny). The karmic path that is not influenced by material elements is also due to past life's karma, just as it is for you. So, materialism, spirituality, and social life are all part of this world. The qualities of Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas are present. But we should not be concerned with that. Follow the Lord's work, the Lord's path, the rules of the scriptures: respect your parents, perform Trikal Sandhya, read the Bhagavat, and chant the name of Madhav. Don't get agitated. Worship your parents as deities, and then, with time, they will change. Jai Jagannath.

Question: Lakshman Sharma from Jaipur asks: When Lord Kalki's avatar manifests, what will be his qualities and characteristics?
Answer: This is a very good question. Your question is an independent one, containing profound elements. Yes, what are the qualities of Lord Kalki, the qualities of God? God comes in Sattvaguna, Rajoguna, and Tamoguna. But no one has ever recognized God, nor will they. A handful of devotees have recognized God in every era, and so it is in this era as well. Yes, I will tell you what is written in the scriptures about the main qualities of Lord Kalki. Around Lord Kalki's body, four clouds and 49 types of winds reside with his body. And just by Lord Kalki's will, he can cause destruction by fire, water, wind, earth, and space. From his body, 49 types of winds can also manifest. Therefore, he will do everything with his willpower. But his main quality is this: at this time, Lord Kalki will establish dharma through the five elements (Panchabhoota). Among the five, the destruction by fire will take the form of a world war. That is a part of Kalki's divine play (leela). Devotees will be protected in it, and sinners will be destroyed. It doesn't matter if a country is the most powerful in the world; the destruction will be based on dharma versus adharma. That is why dharma is greater. There is no way to imagine the power of Lord Kalki; his power is infinite, so it is not possible for us to describe it. But the main quality is that wherever destruction by the five elements happens, where the earth burns through the effect of fire in war, know that nothing happens without the Lord's will. And wherever there is protection, it is by the Lord's grace. The essence of Lord Kalki will remain secret until 2032. After 2032, perhaps Lord Kalki will first become recognizable to a few devotees. Then, gradually, his influence will spread throughout the world. Everyone will believe in him, and then Ram Rajya (the kingdom of Rama) will be established. Jai Jagannath.

Question: Aarti Kumari from Haryana asks: According to the Bhagavat, the Supreme Brahman, Narayana, enveloped by the qualities of Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas, holds countless universes within his body. When it is said that countless universes are situated within him, and he calls himself neither human, nor a living being, nor a soul, then what is his true form? It is also written in the scriptures that it is impossible to know him completely. Still, my mind repeatedly ponders this. Please guide me. Jai Jagannath.
Answer: Look, you have asked a very good question. What is His real body? This is His real body. See, God is formless (nirakar), God is without shape (anakar), and God also comes in a form, in a human body. But the thing is, it is His will. At one place, Tulsidas Maharaj has written, 'Nija ichha nirmit tanudhari' (He assumes a body created by His own will). This means if there are millions of universes in God's body, then when God takes an avatar or comes in a human body, where do those millions of universes go? The answer to this question is that God holds the entire universe, which is beyond the scope of our eyes to see. He operates the entire universe, but the radius of our vision cannot comprehend it. If we, with devotion, emotion, and a sense of surrender, see the supreme essence of Lord Vishnu through self-realization of His actions and inner power, then we will see that His power is in millions of universes, and He holds millions of universes. He operates these millions of universes, which cannot be contained by human eyes but can be contained in human devotion. This means that through devotion, a devotee can experience that true essence. To see it directly, we do not have the 'Gyan Chakshu' (eye of knowledge) that the Lord gave to Arjuna. Through that eye, Arjuna was able to see the form of the entire universe, the true form of the Lord. Then, the Lord also gave the same eye of knowledge to Sanjaya before the Mahabharata war, through which he could perceive and see the Lord's divine play and the righteous act of the Mahabharata war even while being infinitely far away. Therefore, to know this truth, we do not have that capacity. For this reason, by following the path of devotion, only yogis, sages, and rishis could know the attainments of God and His true form through self-realization. And you can do it too. Go into that devotion which the rishis and munis had, then conflict will not arise. And one more thing, when you go with complete faith, is it necessary to follow the Lord? There is the Lord, and by surrendering to the Lord with complete faith, in that very surrender, we will receive the Lord's grace. Yes, this element of the path of knowledge that is in your mind, you can achieve this element only through the path of devotion, the path of austerity, and the path of meditation. To have a direct experience and direct attainment, you will receive the power of God's sacred eye of knowledge. It is possible. Jai Jagannath.

Question: Can we chant Radha's name along with Madhav's name, because the Lord is incomplete without Mother Radha, and it is Mother Radha who grants us loving, protective devotion?
Answer: Look, 'Madhav' is a name in which the names of both Radha Rani and Krishna are chanted together. 'Ma' refers to Brahma Swarupa, Narayani, Vishnu Maya, Sanatani, Mahalakshmi Swarupa, Veda Mata, Gayatri, Radha, and Vasundhara. 'Dhava' means Swami (husband/lord). So, Lord Madhav is the Swami of Radha Rani. This is also described in the Gita Govinda, which contains the essence of the Raas Leela, most beloved to Krishna and Radha. Therefore, by chanting the name 'Madhav', the names of Radha Rani and Krishna are chanted together. If someone's mind is still not satisfied, they can also chant 'Radha Madhav'. That is also acceptable.

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra
Question: Anar Vyas ji from Bor asks: In the first canto of the Bhagavata, the Shukar (Boar) avatar is said to be the second avatar, which occurred before the Meen (Fish) and Kachhap (Tortoise) avatars. Then why, in the sequence given in the Trisandhya, does the Shukar avatar come after the Meen and Kachhap avatars? Why is there this change in the sequence?
Answer: Look, the Meen (Fish) avatar happened during the time of the primordial creation (Adi Sarga), when Lord Maha Vishnu assumed his cosmic form. After that, the Lord created Lord Brahma, Lord Shankar, the eight Vasus, the Digpalas (guardians of directions), and the 14 Manus. Through Brahma, the Saptarishis (seven sages) were also created. When Brahma received the order from the Lord to begin creation, he came to Earth and saw that it was submerged in the great flood. So, the Meen avatar was in the Adi Sarga, meaning the Earth existed before Hiranyakashipu abducted it. When Hiranyakashipu abducted the Earth and took it to the netherworld (Rasatala), the Earth existed before that, and it was that Earth that Hiranyakashipu had stolen. Before that, there was a Brahma Pralaya (dissolution at the end of Brahma's day), during which the Lord took the Meen avatar. After that came the Kumara Sarga, when the Sanakadi Kumaras were born. After that, the Varaha Kalpa came. In the Kachhap (Tortoise) avatar, the churning of the ocean (Samudra Manthan) took place, and the Lord worked for the welfare of the gods. Then, in the Varaha (Boar) avatar, he worked for human civilization on Earth. Therefore, this sequence is correct: Matsya (Fish), Kurma (Tortoise), Varaha (Boar), Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Balarama, Buddha, Kalki. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra
Question: Sanju Kumar from Meerut asks: Shri Krishna is considered the eighth avatar. But in many places, Balaram ji is also called the eighth avatar. Why is this so?
Answer: Look, 'Ete Chamsa Kalah Pumsah Krishnastu Bhagavan Svayam' (All of the below-mentioned incarnations are either plenary portions or portions of the plenary portions of the Lord, but Lord Sri Krishna is the original Personality of Godhead). All the avatars of the Lord who have appeared on Earth are partial incarnations (ansha avatar). But from whom were they created? The Matsya, Kachhap, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Balarama, Buddha, Kalki, Sanakadi Kumaras, Nara-Narayana, Narada Mahamuni, Rishabha avatar, Prithu avatar, Dattatreya avatar—all the avatars that appeared on Earth were created from a part of Lord Krishna. At the time the Dashavatara (ten avatars) concept was composed, the avatar of Maha Vishnu is Krishna; He is Jagannath. Therefore, when a father creates a child, or when God appears in a partial incarnation, God himself does not remain in that avatar form; he remains above, and they are created from him. So, Lord Krishna is God himself. From him, in the form of his partial incarnations, came the 10 avatars, 24 avatars, and countless other avatars. For this reason, the 10 avatars are avatars of Maha Vishnu, and they are partial incarnations of Lord Vishnu, and Lord Maha Vishnu is Krishna. That is why, perhaps in the 10 avatars and 24 avatars, Lord Balaram has been considered an avatar, because all were created from Lord Krishna and later merged back into Lord Krishna. Therefore, Krishna himself is Maha Vishnu, Adi Vishnu, the eternal one. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra

For when Satya Yuga comes after Kali Yuga, the Lord will put those gold coins into circulation. Therefore, gold coins will be in circulation. Paper money will not be used. The iron and brass coins that are currently in use will also not be used. Only gold coins will be used, and people will be able to make purchases in exchange for gold coins. This is written in the Bhavishya Malika scripture. Jai Jagannath.

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra
Question: Akshay bhai ji from Rajasthan asks: Achyutananda ji says that one who recites the Chandi Panjar stuti every morning and evening receives the 'Chobarg phal'. Please clarify this. What is the meaning of this 'Chobarg phal'?
Answer: 'Chobarg' is connected to a worldly element. What is 'Chobarg'? It means a person receives four things from the material world: a royal position, a king-like lifestyle, wealth, and respect. When these four elements are obtained, one gets 'Chobarg'. And regarding the attainment of Moksha (liberation) in the 'Chaturvarga' (four aims of life), in my view, the element of attaining Moksha is something I have read about only in the context of Trikal Sandhya in the scriptures. What Achyutananda ji wrote is that a person who is sad, who has problems, who has troubles related to the material world, will get results by chanting this mantra. But to go to Satya Yuga, Trikal Sandhya is necessary. Jai Jai.

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra

Video Title: L182: भारत पाकिस्तान युद्ध में विजेता कौन होगा? | मालिका में क्या लिखा है? | Pandit Kashinath Mishra
Question: I am eight years old. You said that Lord Krishna was a complete avatar and that's why he wasn't included in the 10 avatars. But Lord Kalki is also a complete avatar, so why is he included in the 10 avatars?

But the scriptures listing the ten avatars were composed in the Dvapara Yuga. Lord Kalki is coming at this time, and he should be worshipped. We should take refuge in him. That is why the sages and Lord Vedavyasa have given Lord Kalki a place as the final avatar among the ten avatars. Another point, your question is very valid. At that time, Lord Balaram also came as Lord Krishna's elder brother. To give respect to his elder brother, all scriptures are composed according to Krishna's will. He instilled this thought in everyone's mind: 'Respect my elder brother.' Therefore, he gave respect to his elder brother and included him in the avatars. But in Kali Yuga, Balaram and Krishna are coming in one body. There is no brother. That is why the Kalki avatar is considered the last of the ten avatars and a complete avatar. Jai Jai Ram.

Video Title: L183: OPERATION SINDOOR के बाद क्या युद्ध होने वाला है ?  | Pandit Kashinath Mishra
